origin.capability_match: Direct $BGT emissions to whitelisted Reward Vaults

attack_surface.data_description: Interaction with Distributor Contract for emission direction.

security_gap.gap_description: The Distributor Contract may be susceptible to transaction spamming or resource exhaustion attacks, preventing legitimate emission directions.

actor_risk.description: An attacker targets the Distributor Contract, affecting all validators including Actor 2.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Inability to direct emissions disrupts the PoL incentive flow and reward distribution.

assumptions: The Distributor Contract (229) can be overloaded by a high volume of valid or invalid transactions.
The underlying network infrastructure (e.g., RPC nodes serving validators) can be targeted to prevent transaction submission to the Distributor Contract.

mitigation.strategy: Implement gas cost mechanisms that penalize spam, design Distributor Contract for high throughput and resilience. Ensure robust RPC infrastructure.

description: An attacker launches a Denial of Service attack against the Distributor Contract or related infrastructure, preventing Validators from successfully submitting their $BGT emission directives.

trace_source.matched_capability: Direct $BGT emissions to whitelisted Reward Vaults
